title: Ask HN: Anyone here bought an abandoned mobile app? What was the process?
summary: High-engagement HN thread on app acquisition. Multiple founders sharing deals: 2-4x revenue multiples, direct outreach to devs. Several commenters mention buying apps for $500–$15K. Strong buyer intent signal.

title: Selling my PDF scanner app — 48K MAU, $340/mo revenue, built 3 years ago [r/SideProject]
summary: Developer selling PDF scanner app with 48K monthly active users and $340/mo ad revenue. Asking 2.5x annual. Android only, built in Java. No broker involved — direct deal possible.

title: Show HN: I turned an abandoned habit tracker into $6K/mo in 4 months
summary: Founder acquired an abandoned habit tracker app for $2,800, relaunched with a paywall, hit $6K/mo in 4 months. Thread details the acquisition process, negotiation, and growth tactics. Strong signal for the acquire+rebuild playbook.

title: My meditation app hit 900K downloads then I stopped updating it 18 months ago. AMA [r/androiddev]
summary: Developer of a 900K-download meditation app doing an AMA about abandoning it. Mentioned he's open to selling. App still gets ~12K downloads/month organically. No asking price stated — classic Partner or Acquire opportunity.

title: AppFlip — Marketplace for buying and selling bootstrapped mobile apps
summary: New marketplace specifically for mobile app acquisitions launching. 127 upvotes, strong community interest. Confirms growing demand for off-market app deals. Competitive signal — move on pipeline now before more buyers enter the market.

title: The graveyard economy: how solo devs are selling apps they can no longer maintain
summary: Long-form analysis of the growing market for abandoned apps. Author interviewed 14 solo developers who sold their apps. Average sale price: $3,200. Most said they would have accepted less if someone had reached out earlier. Key insight: 90% of sellers preferred direct outreach over brokers.
